intro:
There is no information available about the plant "Ebnerella occidentalis". However, I can suggest a similar plant, "Ebnerella occidentalis" might be a misspelling of "Echinacea occidentalis", which is a flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). 

Echinacea occidentalis, also known as Western purple coneflower, is a perennial plant native to the Great Plains in North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of the common cold and flu. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea occidentalis include alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Echinacea occidentalis include teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are available in health food stores and online.
